methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Emelody is a Growth-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Jon Thomas and Elizabeth Johnson that has raised $10M+. The valuation analysis draws from a broader Robotics & Manufacturing sector peer set comprising 1 public company and 8 private firms including Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity.

Only one valuation method proved applicable. Stage-Progression analysis uses the $10M total raised as a baseline proxy and applies a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of Growth-stage advancement, yielding an implied value of $12M. Peer-Median methodology could not be applied; while 7 same-sector peers exist in the Robotics & Manufacturing universe, none met the narrow filtering criteria (same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier) required to produce a reliable 3+ company comparable set. Drawing on all-sector medians without stage and scale alignment would risk introducing material bias.

The triangulated valuation range spans $10.2M (low) to $14M (high), with a median of $12M. This single-method range reflects meaningful uncertainty: the absence of tightly matched comparables in Emelody's immediate peer cohort leaves key questions unresolved around relative unit economics, capital efficiency, and market positioning within the sector. Revenue scale, gross margin profile, and customer concentration would materially inform whether the company trades toward the upper or lower end of this range.
